_README: SOURCE OF TRUTH for fleet tooling — ONE correct way per concern, no forks. The guard (`just fleet-check`, CI .github/workflows/fleet-guard.yml, run with --strict) scans scripts/ for fleet tooling (launch_*, onstart_*, *_chunk_worker, fleet*, *_watch, *cost_watch) and FAILS if a script appears that is not `canonical` here. Add a SUBCOMMAND to `scripts/jobsys/fleet`, or a --mode to the unified Rust worker (onstart_unified → `zenfleet-sweep worker`) — never a new bash fork. The per-metric backfill bash chain (cvvdp/iwssim/omni onstarts + chunk-workers + subdir launchers) and the legacy onstart_v2/v3 were all DELETED 2026-06-25: the Rust unified worker (onstart_unified) already does every metric in one process, so they were dead duplicates. `deprecated` is now empty.
